monetary_values.description: Cost of NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano edge device for on-premises inference

locations.entity: Institute for Applied AI Research, TU Graz

key_facts.fact: Document ID is WP-R&D-2025-007, AlpenTech Technical Whitepaper Series
Seven ML model architectures evaluated ranging from 2.1M to 12.8M parameters
Models evaluated: 1D-CNN, ResNet-1D-18, LSTM-Attention, TCN, Transformer-TS, AlpenPredict-Edge-v1, AlpenPredict-Edge-v2
Four quantization levels tested: FP32, FP16, INT8, and TFLite-INT8
AlpenTech Industrial Benchmark (AIB-42) comprises 2.1 million labeled sensor windows from 42 industrial assets across 5 client installations
Data collection spans 18 months (January 2024 to June 2025)
INT8-quantized AlpenPredict-Edge-v2 achieves 94.2% of cloud baseline's Anomaly Detection F1
AlpenPredict-Edge-v2 INT8 achieves F1 0.912 for anomaly detection and 0.867 for RUL classification
Cloud baseline (FP32): Anomaly F1 0.968, Fault F1 0.943, RUL Acc 0.921
INT8 quantization reduces memory by 73.9% (1,580MB to 412MB) and latency by 65.4% (520ms to 180ms)
Power consumption reduced by 42.3% with INT8 (14.2W to 8.2W)
Vibration-based anomaly detection shows only 1.9% F1 degradation with INT8; temperature-based shows 4.1%
Edge experiments conducted on NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano (8GB RAM) and Siemens SIMATIC IPC227G
AlpenPredict Edge platform processes over 3.2 billion sensor readings per day at five client sites
Accuracy gap between cloud and edge models narrowed to less than 6 percentage points
Internal note contains proprietary distillation parameters and Gabor filter bank configuration filed as trade secret ref TS-2025-0019
Deployment note contains staging environment credentials: Azure Container Registry, MLflow API key, fleet manager admin token
Corresponding author personal email and mobile number exposed: andreas.hofer.priv@gmail.com, +43 660 482 9173

structured_data.title: Predictive Maintenance Using Edge-Deployed ML Models

authors.name: MSc. Florian Ertl
affiliations: Institute for Applied AI Research, TU Graz

key_findings: INT8-quantized lightweight transformer models achieve 94.2% of full-precision cloud-hosted model accuracy on edge devices
AlpenPredict-Edge-v2 (INT8) achieves F1 0.912 for anomaly detection and 0.867 for RUL classification on NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano
INT8 quantization reduces memory by 73.9%, latency by 65.4%, and power by 42.3% compared to FP32
Vibration-based anomaly detection is more robust to quantization (1.9% degradation) than temperature-based (4.1%)
Sovereign on-premises predictive maintenance is practically viable with accuracy gap under 6 percentage points
AlpenPredict Edge processes 3.2 billion sensor readings per day at five production client sites
contributions: Comprehensive evaluation of 7 ML architectures across 4 quantization levels on industrial predictive maintenance using real-world AIB-42 benchmark
Detailed analysis showing quantization impact is highly task- and sensor-type-dependent
Production-ready AlpenPredict Edge framework for automated training, quantization, and OTA deployment to industrial edge gateways

summary: AlpenTech GmbH R&D Division technical whitepaper (WP-2025-007) presenting edge-deployed ML models for sovereign predictive maintenance. Seven architectures evaluated across four quantization levels on AIB-42 benchmark (2.1M labeled sensor windows from 42 industrial assets at 5 DACH client sites). Key finding: INT8-quantized AlpenPredict-Edge-v2 achieves 94.2% of cloud baseline accuracy (F1 0.912 anomaly detection) on NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano with 73.9% memory reduction and 65.4% latency reduction. AlpenPredict Edge platform processes 3.2B sensor readings/day at five production sites. Contains sensitive internal notes with proprietary model parameters (trade secret TS-2025-0019), staging environment credentials (Azure ACR, MLflow API key, fleet admin JWT), and author personal contact information.
